chunk_summary: Defines core IYCF indicators (MMF, MDD, MAD) and counseling thresholds for culturally effective infant feeding in India, emphasizing structured 24-hour recall and actionable dietary adjustments.
learning_objectives: Understand the definitions and thresholds for core IYCF indicators: MMF, MDD, and MAD.
Learn how to conduct a structured 24-hour dietary recall for infants and young children.
Identify culturally appropriate strategies to improve feeding frequency and dietary diversity.
Apply WHO/UNICEF guidelines to local Indian reporting formats and counseling practices.
key_facts: Minimum Meal Frequency (MMF): Breastfed children need ≥2 feeds/day (6–8 months) or ≥3 feeds/day (9–23 months); non-breastfed children need ≥4 feeds/day.
Minimum Dietary Diversity (MDD): At least 5 of 8 food groups in the past 24 hours, per updated WHO/UNICEF guidelines.
Minimum Acceptable Diet (MAD): Achieved when MMF and MDD are met, plus ≥2 milk feeds for non-breastfed children.
Structured 24-hour recall: Maps intake to MMF and MDD targets for actionable counseling.
Indian reporting formats may use older 4-of-7 food group cut-offs; counsel toward the 5-of-8 goal.
reference_quote: Minimum dietary diversity (MDD) uses the updated WHO/UNICEF definition: at least 5 of 8 food groups in the past 24 hours.
document_key_topics: Core IYCF indicator definitions (MMF, MDD, MAD)
Structured dietary recall for feeding assessment
Culturally effective counseling strategies in India
